rationale: Gate-integrity attack. (b) tally-as-authority and (c) verify-read-only are SOUND: Non-Goals pin `complete` as the sole authoritative enforcer and tally's exit as a reporting convenience — `tally && complete` cannot launder because complete re-runs its own gate over fresh facts (TOCTOU-safe); R2 forbids verify writes with a falsifier+test+git-clean proof. (a) `create --round N+1` laundering is DEFEATED by the existing round-scoping in tally.go (LatestRound from filenames, latest-round-only counting, RoundMismatch block) plus the staleness check — bumping the round with stale prior-round verdicts BLOCKS; R1 preserves prior verdicts and re-resolves the SHA, faithfully. But (d) inverse-108 lands on R6: unlike R5 (pinned to the named tested `contextpack.ParseSpec` with exact severity), R6 leaves the ADR-reference EXTRACTION from `## ADR Touchpoints` unspecified. The spec's OWN touchpoints prose (line 36) names ADR-0040 (absent from the store) and ADR-0030 (not a citation); a naive `ADR-\d{4}` token-scan R6 would extract ADR-0040 and FAIL 110's own spec-approve — an internal contradiction, and gameable both ways (prose dodge vs prose false-positive). Fixable by pinning R6's extraction rule.
concrete_changes_required: R6: pin the ADR-reference EXTRACTION rule for `## ADR Touchpoints`. Specify references are ONLY the anchored markdown-link touchpoint bullets (`- [ADR-XXXX](<path>): ...`), and that bare `ADR-####` prose mentions (e.g. 'per ADR-0040', '(ADR-0030)') are NOT extracted or resolved. Name the honest boundary (integrity-checks the anchored citations; an unlinked reference is out of scope), mirroring how R5 is bounded by contextpack.ParseSpec's bullet extraction. Add a falsifier: 'Falsified if a bare prose ADR-#### mention inside `## ADR Touchpoints` is extracted — it would fail 110's own spec-approve on the ADR-0040/ADR-0030 prose mentions.'

Reconcile the spec's OWN §ADR Touchpoints self-inconsistency: line 36 asserts 'citing ADR-0040 would fail the check this spec models' while itself writing 'ADR-0040' (absent) and 'ADR-0030' (not a citation) as prose inside that section. Add an explicit note that these are prose mentions, not anchored citations, and thus out of R6's extraction — or restructure so the spec unambiguously passes its own R6.

Acceptance criterion (line 93 / TestValidateSpec_RejectsMissingADRTouchpoint): add cases that PIN the extraction boundary — (a) an anchored `- [ADR-9999](...)` to a nonexistent ADR FAILS with a recovery line; (b) a bare unlinked prose 'per ADR-9999' inside the section does NOT fail; (c) a spec shaped like 110 (linked citations to existing ADRs + prose mentions of a not-yet-existing ADR) PASSES. This proves R6 cannot be gamed in either direction.

R1: specify the BRIEF<->panel.json SHA relationship on re-panel. R1 writes the resolved commit SHA into BOTH panel.json (reviewed_head_sha) and the BRIEF stub, but only pins the panel.json round+SHA co-bump on `create --round N+1` — it is silent on whether the BRIEF's machine-derived round/SHA is re-written (and how it avoids clobbering the skill's round-N judgment prose). A stale BRIEF SHA can misdirect round-(N+1) reviewers while the gate (which reads only panel.json) passes. Either drop the SHA from the BRIEF stub (single source = panel.json) OR specify create re-writes the BRIEF's machine-derived region on re-panel, preserving judgment placeholders, with a falsifier.

R1 (minor): note that for a `--bead <id>` panel, `--target` is expected to resolve to `bead/<beadID>` — the ref the gate's staleness check rev-parses. State that a divergent `--target` only ever fails safe (false-BLOCK, never false-PASS), so `create` cannot be pointed at an off-branch SHA the gate would then accept.
findings: SOUND — tally as authority (b): Non-Goals + R7a keep PanelGateDecision the single home and `complete` the only enforcer; tally/verify are read-side previews reusing the same decision+facts. `tally && complete` cannot bypass because complete independently re-resolves facts (staleness/dirty) at merge time. No new decision semantics, no second enforcement point. No change needed.

SOUND — verify read-only (c): R2 forbids any write, with a dedicated falsifier, the TestPanelVerify_MatchesGateAndWritesNothing criterion, and a `git status clean` manual proof. tally is also print-only; the consolidated file is authored by the skill (R8), not the verb — no divergent cache/summary file is introduced by verify or tally. (Caveat folded into the BRIEF-SHA change above, which is a create-side write.)

SOUND — create --round N+1 laundering (a): verified against internal/panel/tally.go. LatestRound is filename-derived; verdicts are tallied for the latest round ONLY (earlier-round files contribute nothing); RoundMismatch (panel.json round != filename round) Blocks. So re-resolving the SHA and bumping the round while leaving prior-round verdicts untouched yields a RoundMismatch/incomplete BLOCK until fresh round-(N+1) verdicts land — laundering is defeated. Pointing create at an arbitrary/off-branch SHA cannot false-pass the bead gate, which rev-parses bead/<id> independently (fails safe).

DEFECT — R6 extraction unspecified + spec self-inconsistency (d, inverse-108): R5 is pinned to the named, tested contextpack.ParseSpec with exact severity (confirmed against ownership_resolve.go: bare-name kept verbatim, path-like zero/ambiguous owner = error — matches R5). R6 has NO equivalent named extractor for `## ADR Touchpoints`, and spec 097 R2 explicitly REMOVED touchpoint-prose scraping — so R6 re-introduces a brand-new, unspecified prose parser. Confirmed empirically: ADR-0040 is absent from the store and 'ADR-0040' appears as prose in the spec's own §ADR Touchpoints (line 36), so a token-scan R6 would fail 110's own spec-approve. This is exactly the 'formatting a machine extracts but humans misread' class the spec exists to close. Primary reason for REQUEST_CHANGES.

OBSERVATION — the spec's own §Impacted Domains (lines 31-32) deliberately uses prose (not bullets) to keep core/context-system/execution out of ParseSpec's extraction. This is benign for R5 (those domains change no source, and R5 only validates extracted domains at the downstream severity), but it is the SAME extraction-boundary reliance that makes R6's unspecified boundary dangerous. R5's boundary is safe only because it is anchored to a named tested parser; R6 must be given the same anchoring.
